name: 21 Gun Salute
description: To obtain the 21 Gun Salute bar, a student must drink 21 glasses of any assortment of beverages available at any of the cafeterias, using the glasses they provide at the caf. It is widely accepted that each drink must be a different drink. All beverages must be consumed within one sitting and within one hour. Vomiting while attempting the 21 Gun Salute will void the attempt (and probably get you thrown out, too!). Following the consumption of the 21 glasses, the person attempting the bar must then get to the other cafeteria and touch the wall, still within the original hour.

name: 21st Century
description: To complete this bar, a person must complete 21 Gun Salute followed by Century Club in the same day.

name: 21st Century Wizard
description: Complete 21 Gun Salute, Century Club, and Wizard within 24 hours. The 24 hours do not need to be the same calendar day.

name: 40 Beers
description: 40 Beers involves drinking 40 beers in a period of 24 hours. During the period you cannot puke, but you may sleep. One may choose to drink a number "n" say the first night, sleep for a few hours and drink the remaining 40-n beers, as long as it occurs within 24 hours of commencing.Old tradition dictates the first 15 be done at ritual, ideally with turbo.

name: Advance To Goodes
description: In a group of 3+ in formal attire (suits and ties) or GPAs (still with ties), must challenge passing commies to, start, and finish a game of Monopoly in the front lobby of Goodes Hall.Some play with the rules that someone in your group must win the game of Monopoly for it to count. If a commie wins, you don't get the bar.

name: Beer Belt
description: You have 12 hours to attach every beer can that you finish to your beer belt (a strand of tape [with the sticky side facing outwards]). Make sure they are all secure. Use as much tape as necessary. Must have beers going all around the waist to accomplish the task.

name: Beerio Kart
description: Beerio Kart (also known as Grand Pre, KUI, Don't Drink and Drive, and others) is a set of drinking games to be played alongside Mario Kart.There are many variations to this game however the core element is that you must drink an entire beer within the duration of a single Mario Kart race. However, you cannot drink your beer and drive your kart (have your hands on the controller) at the same time. Addtional Rules: Any additional rules or modifications can be added to the core game. Some additonal rules are listed below.Winner of race is exempt from drinking (once only) An additional shot of beer is taken for being hit by an item Last place in a race takes a shot of hard liquor Players must pull over on the right side of the road and come to a complete stop before drinking

name: Beerlin Wall
description: Completely cover your Don's door in beer cans while they are in their room.Note that all alcohol must be drunk in one night, but can be drunk by anyone willing to contribute.Originally called Beerlyn Wall to commerate the Don it was first completed on, named Lyn.

name: Brew Master
description: Brew Master bar is similar to Tap Master only it involves making either beer or wine (or moonshine we don't discriminate) in your rez room and then consuming it. It must all be done inside your rez room and be enough to get at least 6 people drunk.

name: Century Club
description: To obtain this bar, a student must drink one shot of beer every minute for one hundred consecutive minutes. Vomiting during the attempt will nullify the attempt.

name: Clark Decagaon
description: Take 10 shots of hard liquor or finish a pitcher in 10 minutes at Ritual at Clark Hall Pub. If drinking the pitcher, chugging is frowned upon by the StuCons. If doing the shots, you will likely be allowed to finish but will be removed from the premises immediately following completion.NOTE: This is not a bar, this is a patch in the shape of the decagon.

name: Cuffed
description: It's cuffing season baby! Spend 24 straight hours handcuffed to your best bud. In said time you must match each other drink for drink. Bonus points for a bottle of wine each, because that shit's romantic. No removing the handcuffs for any reason, so don't break the seal early. Makeshift handcuffs are allowed but make them sturdy, because you can't bear to be without your other half for even a second. Chaining more than 2 people together qualifies you for Cuffed^x

name: Drink to the Future
description: Drink to the Future is a drinking bar. To get this bar you must watch all three films in the Back to the Future trilogy back to back. Everytime someone says "Doc", "Great Scott!", or "Back to the Future", take a drink from your alcohol of choice (beer, mixed drink etc.)

name: DUSTED
description: This stands for "Drink an Unsuspecting Small Town Establishment Dry". To get this bar, one must go with a group and attempt to drink a bar in a smaller surrounding town of Kingston dry. It is widely accepted that the bar does not have to be drank "dry", so long as a valiant attempt made.

name: Fellowship
description: The premise of the bar is to download all three of the Lord of the Rings: Extended Editions, and watch them consecutively (it takes approx. 12+ hours) and drinking every time anyone says the word "ring", "frodo" and every time there is a panoramic shot. Some versions also include smoking up any time any character lights up their pipe or cigarettes and drinking every time "your" character drinks and or does something sweet (ie. Gandalf's scene with the Balrog). All of these requirements have their own discretions.

name: Frost Week
description: Frost Week is for those brave enough to face the elements in the most epic of clothing, The GPA.To obtain "Frost Week" one must don sandals (add socks for 10/10 eng fashion), a bathing suit a dank AF muscle shirt (or shirtless), and of course, their Golden Party Armour. If you're feeling adventurous you can also wear aviators for extra style points and general badass-ery.They must wear this from the time of their first class of the first week of the winter semester, to the time of their final class of that same week (i.e. 8:30 am Monday to 5:30 pm Friday).

name: God of the Sea
description: Participants must create a beer can trident, standing as tall as themselves (Like a wizard staff with to branches coming off of the main tower.) Estimated 16-18 tallboys.for a nice trident depending on the person.The first and last beer must be shot-gunned while standing on the pier and ALL must be consumed in the time it takes the tide to change. (Usually around 11 hours but for the exact day it can be easily googled at online soures). Only once this has been completed will you be a true God Of The Sea.

name: Heineken Hammer
description: The participant must finish a Heineken Mini Keg (5L, approx. 14-15 bottles). They then attach the keg to a sturdy handle (baseball ball, 2×4, hockey stick, etc.) to create said "Heineken Hammer."

name: Hero of Canton
description: In a similar manner to every other drinking bar involving sitting in one position for twelve hours drinking; Hero of Canton requires that you steadily drink your way through Joss Whedon's Firefly collection. Please note that this includes the feature film Serenity. Refer to website for standard rules.

name: Home For a Rest
description: AKA: Home For A Rekt. Home For A Rest is a quick yet disgustingly difficult bar to achieve. This bar can be attempted with a group of people as to make it more fun. To obtain this bar, one must start by playing the song "Home For A Rest" by Spirit Of The West. Once the song gets f***ing rowdy (0:46) then the challenge begins. While on your knees with both hands behind your back, you must drink three beers (1,065mL) split amongst four cups. NO SPILLING!! Once the cups are empty, the challenge truly begins. Still, with hands behind your back, you must take a shot of "cement mixer". All must be completed before the song ends.

name: ILCBO
description: For ILCBO, a small group of 4-5 people must alternate taking shots at every single group room in the ILC (one per group room). as of the 2017-2018 academic year there are 39 rooms.

name: Isengard
description: Isengard is a variation of wizard. Rather than build one's height in beer, a group of people (however many necessary) work together to build a tower of beer the height of Waldron Tower. The tower must go from the ground of the basement (Floor 0) to the roof of the top floor (Floor 11), for a total height of approximately 120 feet. This feat of construction must be completed in a single night: all beer must be drunk on the night of construction. You are eligible for a bar if you either contribute a significant amount of cans to the cause or help construct the final tower. Drinking the beer is not the difficult part; the real challenge is not getting caught.

name: Iron Curtain
description: You must build an "Iron Curtain" that separates Lenny East from Lenny West, along the first floor lobby. Must be aprox head height (6 feet). The difficulty comes from size, support, and not getting caught. Bonus points if you cover the east side with communist memorabilia. Same time limit applies as for Beerlin Wall (12 hours). All cans must be drank within this time limit. Note: playing USSR anthem remix is highly recommended:

name: Iron Throne
description: This bar is based on HBO's emmy-winning show A Game of Thrones. Recommended group size is 7-10 people. Starting from Season 1 Episode 1 and ending at episode 9 "Baelor" (at Ned Starks exit)---.

name: Last Man Standing
description: Also known as Last Woman Standing and Last One Standing. In your first year after writing the final winter exam, be the last remaining person/small group of people to be slamming in front of Clark Hall Pub. Beginning with Sci'18 it became the tradition of slamming your Pole Time. You must slam your full Pole Time. In the event that frosh are forced to stop slamming due to the University, Engineering Society, or City of Kingston, frosh are required to continue slamming elsewhere for the remainder of the time in order to achieve the bar. A break is permitted during the walk from Clark Hall Patio to the new location (e.g The Pier). This change was due to other faculties complaining about slamming taking so long that it would disrupt their own examinations. Taking extended or frequent breaks during the slam nullifies the attempt. Note: If you at any point stopped slamming, didn't keep pace with upper years, or half-assed your slams, especially during the last half hour, you do not merit this bar.

name: Lumberjack
description: Starting October 1st or earlier, do not shave your beard or cut your hair (minor trimming allowed) for the rest of the semester. Then, wear a plaid shirt and a toque to your Engineering Geology and the Biosphere exam. After the exam you may choose to return looking like you have your life together (looking being the imperative word). Wearing blue denim and steel toe boots is optional but highly recommended. For females, as they typically cannot grow facial hair and have long hair, are to not shave their arms, legs, or armpits as an equivalence.

name: Naked Knights
description: Naked Knights involves streaking (usually around the city block where you are) while wearing only a medieval Knight helmet and shoes. (One can make the helmet out of beer)

name: No Hablo
description: For the iLliTEraTe and English impaired people who failed the English proficiency test. If you "fail" the test because you skipped it in order to get this bar, you don't get the bar, you're just an idiot.

name: Party Star
description: Play an entire 50 turn game of Mario Party (must be 4 players, no CPUs). Every time you are not the winner of a mini-game, you must take a drink. Additionally, every time you either lose a star or get a star stolen you must take 1 shot of hard liquor (at least 40%). It is not required that you drink beer, mixed drinks are allowed. No puking and no taking a break and finishing the game later.

name: Raiders
description: Similar to Fellowship and Drink to the Future. Watch the 3 original Indiana Jones movies (Raiders of the Lost Ark, Temple of Doom, and The Last Crusade). The order doesn't really matter but if you want to do it chronologically (in the story, not release dates) it should go Temple, Raiders, and then Crusade.

name: Ritual
description: Attend Ritual at Clark Hall Pub 10 times in a row within one semester (does not carry over Winter break) or 14 times in total within an academic year (carries over Winter break). On your 10th/14th attendance, you must bring the physical Ritual bar with you and place it in any drinks you consume. If the bar is snatched from your drink you must immediately finish whatever is remaining in the container. All Rituals count towards attendance, including special Rituals. Note: some alum lay claim to a Ritual^2, consisting of 100 total rituals before their final departure from Queen's.

name: Rocky Road
description: Taking a tub of ice cream from Leonard cafeteria or Ban Righ caf and run it to Miller Hall. Additionally Lazy now has ice-cream for those that consider themselves beyond brave. (2016-2017) You will be one of the least lame frosh if after you arrive at Miller Hall you leave it on the door steps. Bring spoons for your new found friends comprised of geologists and geo engineers. People in Geo need some lovin'n so be a good person and share the love and the ice cream.

name: Slam It Home
description: Get together with your fellow Queen's engineers and do a jacket slam at your hometown university.

name: Smashed Bros
description: In Super Smash Bros, each player is given 100 lives. Every time your character dies, you must take a shot of beer. Traditonally this is done in SSB Melee on the map Final Destination.

name: Smooth Criminal
description: Recieve a level 3 documentation in residence without getting kicked out of res. Getting this on purpose or on the last day/week is just plain stupid.

name: Squeaky Clean
description: To obtain the bar "Squeaky Clean", one must take a shower in every named residence building. Squeaky Clean must be completed in the span of 7 hours.

name: Tap Master
description: Host a rez kegger. Keg must be full size (58.6L) and brought in, consumed and returned without being caught. Only the person who's room the keg resides in receives the bar. In the case of the kegger being held in a single plus room (Watts Legget Brant Smith) the bar will go to the person organizing the event.

name: Tir Na Nog
description: A bar created for those who frequent Tír nan Óg (an Irish pub downtown) thursday nights. This bar is similar to the old interpretation of Clark Decagon, in that there aren't set rules for getting it, rather just if you feel you deserve it.

name: Totem
description: The idea is similar to Wizard. Drink an entire Heineken mini-keg by yourself, then add it to a stack of other mini-kegs to make a totem pole. The stack must be a minimum of 10 mini-kegs high in order to qualify for the bar, but feel free to make it taller. It is recommended that you have a "Totem" party, where each person invited brings their own mini-keg. Duct tape is also recommended.

name: White Wizard
description: White Wizard is obtained by completing Wizard during Fellowship . Mandatory to choose Gandalf as your character.

name: Wizard
description: Within a 12 hour time frame, you must drink your height in beers. After every beer you finish, you must tape it to the bottom of the next beer you are about to drink, before you start drinking it. You are not allowed to puke until you have completed your staff. Bonus points for keeping your staff free-standing.
